program_name: Artificial intelligence for science and technology
degree_class: LM-91 - Methods and techniques for the information society
academic_requirements: Applicants must hold a Bachelor's degree, or an equivalent international qualification. The official UniMi 2026/2027 manifesto states that 180 credits are required for admission. UniMi also requires basic knowledge of computer science, mathematics, statistics and physics, documented by at least 30 CFU in the following areas: INF/01, ING-INF/05, INGINF/03, MAT/01 to MAT/09, SECS-S/01 to SECS-S/06, and FIS/01 to FIS/08. As an open-admission master's programme, admission is subject to verification of curricular requirements and assessment of the candidate's personal preparation by the Admission Board.
language_requirements: English at B2 level or higher is required for admission. UniMi's accepted-certificate rules state that accepted English certificates must certify Reading, Writing, Listening and Speaking, and must have been earned no more than 3 years before submission (2 years for TOEFL).
application_deadline_eu: [uncertain] UniMi does not publish a programme-specific 2026/2027 EU deadline on its own page for this joint degree. The official UniMi open-admission master's page states that applications open on 22 January 2026 and that application deadlines for open-admission master's programmes fall between July and October 2026, depending on the degree programme.
application_deadline_non_eu: 30 April 2026 for non-EU students residing outside Italy who require a visa. UniMi also states that the Universitaly pre-enrolment application must be submitted by 31 July 2026.
required_documents: [uncertain] UniMi does not publish a programme-specific 2026/2027 admission notice on its own domains for this joint degree; the concrete documents below are those officially required by UniMi's open-admission and international-enrolment procedures that apply to the programme.

Self-certification of university studies showing exams taken, credits, and Scientific Disciplinary Sectors, for applicants who are graduands or graduates of a university other than the University of Milan.
Proof of the 30 euro admission fee payment.

Evidence of the required academic background, including the credits in the listed subject areas; for applicants from other universities this is covered through the self-certification/transcript of studies.
English B2 certificate or other officially accepted proof of English proficiency, if used to document the language requirement.

For applicants with qualifications obtained abroad during online enrolment: high school diploma; Bachelor's degree (or equivalent); CIMEA Statements of Comparability and Verification, or Dichiarazione di Valore in loco; for Lisbon Recognition Convention countries, Statement of Correspondence via ARDI or Diploma Supplement together with Statement of Verification.
For applicants with qualifications obtained abroad: identity document and fiscal code.
For non-EU citizens: copy of valid residence permit, if already resident in Italy.
For non-EU students residing abroad: receipt of the Universitaly pre-application, request for a residence permit, study visa, and any certificate of competence in Italian where applicable.
entry_exam_or_test: [uncertain] UniMi does not publish a separate programme-specific entrance exam, interview calendar, or admission notice on its own domains for A.Y. 2026/2027. The official programme page states only that admission includes verification of curricular requirements and assessment of personal preparation; UniMi's general open-admission rules add that this assessment may be documentary only or may include an interview or preparation test if required by the programme committee.

source_quotes.quote: This master's program is open admission.
Admission is subject to verification of the curricular requirements and assessment of the candidate's personal preparation.
candidates must have earned at least 30 credits (CFU) ... INF/01, ING-INF/05, INGINF/03
candidates must prove English language proficiency of B2 level or higher.
Applications to open-admission Master’s degree programmes are open from 22 January 2026.
Application deadlines are between July and October, depending on each degree programme.
Non-EU students VISA applicants can apply for admission until 30 April 2026.
must be presented on the Universitaly website within 31 July 2026
receipt of the admission fee payment; ... Degree (Bachelor Sc. or equivalent)
receipt of the Universitaly pre-application; ... study Visa;
Certificates must have been earned for no more than 3 years ... (2 years for TOEFL)

uncertainty_notes: UniMi's own programme page for this joint degree explicitly redirects admissions and enrolment details to the University of Milano-Bicocca portal. Because the user requested UniMi-only sources, I did not use the Bicocca page and therefore could not extract a programme-specific EU deadline or a programme-specific admission notice from UniMi domains.

A UniMi 2026/2027 manifesto PDF exists on apps.unimi.it and was used as the official PDF source, but it is only a short programme-description/teaching-structure file and does not publish application windows or a programme-specific document checklist.

For EU applicants, UniMi publishes only the general open-admission master's timetable on its own domains: applications open on 22 January 2026 and close between July and October 2026 depending on the programme. UniMi does not publish the exact AIST deadline on its own page.

UniMi does not publish a programme-specific entrance test or interview schedule for this joint degree on its own domains. The only official UniMi wording is verification of curricular requirements plus assessment of personal preparation; the possibility of an interview/test comes from UniMi's general open-admission procedure.

The required-documents list is concrete and official, but it is assembled from UniMi's general open-admission and international-enrolment pages because UniMi does not publish a programme-specific 2026/2027 admission notice for this joint degree on its own domains.
